rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component or implementation scope as determined by the current requirement
Preserve existing AADL architecture by only inserting annex blocks, not modifying other elements
Use visible identifiers from the current requirement when available
Do not insert annex blocks that reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the appropriate scope based on the requirement context
Preserve the existing AADL architecture during annex insertion
Use visible identifiers when available for annex block content
Avoid referencing subcomponent ports in component type contracts
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};`
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` for valid AGREE syntax
Place assumptions and guarantees in the appropriate scope based on the requirement context
anti_patterns: Do not insert annex blocks that reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not modify existing AADL architecture during annex insertion
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
AADL property names as AGREE variables
